task_completion.description: Configure Stellarium location to Tranquility Base on the Moon
task_completion.visual_evidence: Stellarium 'Location' window is visible. The 'Planet' or 'Body' is set to 'Moon'. The latitude is set to approximately 0.674° N (or N 0°40') and longitude to 23.473° E (or E 23°28').

task_completion.description: Configure Stellarium date and time to the Apollo 11 landing time
task_completion.visual_evidence: Stellarium 'Date and Time' window is visible, showing the date as 1969-07-21 and the time as 02:56:00.
The sky in Stellarium appears completely black (no atmospheric scattering) even when the Sun is visible, or the 'Atmosphere' toggle button in the lower toolbar is visibly turned off.

task_completion.description: Capture a screenshot centered on the Earth
task_completion.visual_evidence: Stellarium view shows the Earth selected (with a reticle around it) and centered on the screen, with information text about Earth visible.

task_completion.description: Capture a screenshot centered on the Sun
task_completion.visual_evidence: Stellarium view shows the Sun selected (with a reticle around it) and centered on the screen, with information text about the Sun visible.

task_completion.description: Create the reference document with required notes
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal is visible showing the contents of 'apollo_vr_reference.txt' on the Desktop. The text includes the location (Moon / Tranquility Base), date (July 21, 1969), and confirms screenshots of Earth and Sun were taken.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Stellarium application
integrity.visual_evidence: The Stellarium application interface is visibly open and running in the screenshots, demonstrating actual interaction with the software rather than just editing configuration files blindly.

integrity.description: Agent did not use a web browser to fake screenshots
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent downloading images of the Earth or Sun from a web browser to fake the screenshots.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely created the reference text file
integrity.visual_evidence: The reference text file is seen being created or edited within the system (e.g., using a text editor or terminal commands).

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Planet: Moon. Target Coordinates: 0.674° N, 23.473° E (or 0.01176 rad N, 0.40968 rad E). Target Date/Time: July 21, 1969, 02:56:00 UTC. Atmosphere must be disabled (black sky). Reference file path: ~/Desktop/apollo_vr_reference.txt. Required screenshots: Earth and Sun.
